notes: No state paid family leave. Federal FMLA may apply (12 unpaid weeks).

California PFL pays a tiered 70%/90% under SB 951: 90% of weekly wages for lower earners (up to ~70% of SAWW), 70% above. Max $1,765/wk for 2026. SDI covers pregnancy disability (typically 6-8 weeks for vaginal / 8-10 for C-section).
Colorado FAMLI pays 90% of weekly wages up to 50% of state AWW, then 50% above. Max $1,381.45/wk for 2026. Up to 4 additional perinatal weeks for complications.
CT Paid Leave pays 95% up to 40x minimum wage, 60% above. Max $1,016.40/wk for 2026. 2 additional weeks for serious health condition during pregnancy.
Delaware PFML launched January 2026. Parental leave is up to 12 weeks at 80% wage replacement; medical / caregiver / qualifying-exigency leaves are capped at 6 weeks per 24-month period. Max $900/wk.
DC Paid Family Leave: up to 12 weeks at 90% of wages. Max $1,190/wk (effective September 28, 2025).
No state paid family leave. State employees only have a limited program.
Hawaii has Temporary Disability Insurance (covers pregnancy/recovery) but no paid bonding leave yet.
Illinois requires 5 paid sick days per year (2024+). Chicago has a paid leave ordinance.
Maine Paid Family and Medical Leave begins benefit payments May 1, 2026. Tiered: 90% up to 50% of SAWW, 66% above. Max $1,198.84/wk through 6/30/26 (recalculates annually).

Maryland Family and Medical Leave Insurance was scheduled for 2026 but the launch was delayed: contributions begin January 1, 2027 and benefit payments begin January 3, 2028. For 2026 only federal FMLA applies.
Massachusetts PFML: 12 weeks family / 20 weeks medical / 26 combined. Tiered 80% up to 50% of SAWW, 50% above. Max $1,230.39/wk for 2026.
Minnesota Paid Leave launched January 1, 2026. Up to 12 weeks family / 12 weeks medical / 20 combined. Tiered replacement; max $1,423/wk for 2026.
Voluntary state program; opt-in coverage available through MetLife.
12 weeks of paid family leave at 85% of wages, max $1,119/wk for 2026. Separate Temporary Disability Insurance covers pregnancy recovery.
12 weeks paid family leave at 67% of average weekly wage, max $1,228.53/wk for 2026. NY Disability Benefits Law also covers pregnancy recovery.
State employees have 8 weeks paid parental leave; no statewide private-sector program.
Up to 12 weeks paid (14 with pregnancy complications). Tiered 100% up to 65% of SAWW, then 50% above. Max $1,636.56/wk through July 5, 2026.
Temporary Caregiver Insurance: 8 weeks paid bonding leave (up from 7 effective January 1, 2026). Max $1,103/wk for 2026. TDI covers pregnancy disability separately.
Voluntary state PFL through The Hartford launched 2023; opt-in coverage.
No state paid family leave. State employees have 8 weeks paid parental leave.
Washington: up to 12 weeks family / 12 weeks medical / 16 combined (plus 2 perinatal). Tiered 90% up to 50% of SAWW, 50% above. Max $1,647/wk for 2026.
